package user_test

import (
	"context"
	"testing"

	"connectrpc.com/connect"

	pb "example.com/proj/gen/services/user/v1"
	"example.com/proj/handlers/user"
	"example.com/proj/pkg/app"
)

// TestHandlers exercises each RPC against the service.
func TestHandlers(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	svc := app.NewTestUser(t)
	ctx := context.Background()

	tests := []struct {
		name string
		call func() error
	}{
		{
			name: "GetCurrentUser",
			call: func() error {
				_, err := svc.GetCurrentUser(ctx, connect.NewRequest(&pb.GetCurrentUserRequest{}))
				return err
			},
		},
		{
			name: "UpdateProfile",
			call: func() error {
				_, err := svc.UpdateProfile(ctx, connect.NewRequest(&pb.UpdateProfileRequest{}))
				return err
			},
		},
	}

	for _, tt := range tests {
		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			t.Parallel()
			err := tt.call()
			if err != nil {
				t.Logf("%s returned error: %v", tt.name, err)
			}
		})
	}
}

// TestAuthorizerDenyByDefault verifies fail-closed default authz.
func TestAuthorizerDenyByDefault(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	a := user.NewAuthorizer()
	if err := a.Can(context.Background(), nil, "", ""); err == nil {
		t.Fatal("expected default authorizer to deny")
	}
}
